2606.20532.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
2606.20532.paper_summary: Style-captioned text-to-speech systems use natural language to control voice characteristics, but how individual words influence acoustic output remains unclear. Understanding this is critical for diagnosing failure modes and improving controllability in expressive TTS. We propose cross-attention attribution for speech diffusion models, adapting the DAAM framework to the speech domain for the first time, and apply it to CapSpeech-TTS. Our method extracts per-token heatmaps across 25 layers and 24 ODE steps. We analyze 3,600 (style caption, text transcript) combinations comprising 120 style captions conditioning the generation of 30 text transcripts each, revealing how caption tokens shape waveforms. Results show: (1) style tokens have lower temporal variance than content/function tokens, confirming global conditioning; (2) style attention correlates with F0 and energy; (3) style conditioning peaks in early steps and deep layers; (4) attention entropy reaches its minimum at layer 17, co-occurring with the style importance peak, indicating maximal network selectivity at the most style-critical stage. This is the first study of how natural language influences cross-attention in speech diffusion models
2606.20532.paper_summary_zh: 風格標題的文本到語音系統使用自然語言來控制聲音特徵，但單個單詞如何影響聲學輸出仍不清楚。理解這一點對於診斷故障模式和提高表達性TTS的可控性至關重要。我們提出了語音擴散模型的交叉注意力歸因，首次將DAAM框架調整到語音領域，並將其應用於CapSpeech-TTS。我們的方法提取了25層和24個ODE步驟的每個標記熱圖。我們分析了3,600個（風格標題，文本轉錄）組合，這些組合由120個風格標題條件生成每個30個文本轉錄，揭示了標題標記如何塑造波形。結果顯示：（1）風格標記的時間變異性低於內容/功能標記，確認了全局條件；（2）風格注意力與F0和能量相關；（3）風格條件在早期步驟和深層達到峰值；（4）注意力熵在第17層達到最小值，與風格重要性峰值同時出現，表明在最關鍵的風格階段網絡選擇性達到最大。這是第一項研究自然語言如何影響語音擴散模型中交叉注意力的研究。
2606.20532.author: Nityanand Mathur et.al.
2606.20532.authors: Nityanand Mathur, Hamees Sayed, Wasim Madha, Apoorv Singh, Sameer Khurana, Akshat Mandloi, Sudarshan Kamath
